title: quercus × brittonii
intro: Quercus × brittonii, also known as the Britton oak or Britton's oak, is a hybrid of Quercus alba and Quercus coccinea, a deciduous tree native to eastern North America. The medicinal properties of Quercus × brittonii lie in its bark, which contains flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Quercus × brittonii can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or extract, and is often used in traditional herbal medicine to support overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
